name: empty_map_nonempty_vec
code:
proof fn phi_1_empty_map_nonempty_vec(m: CKeyHashMap)
    requires
        m@ === Map::<AbstractKey, Seq<u8>>::empty(),
    ensures
        m.spec_to_vec()@.len() > 0,
{
}
reason: An empty map should never produce a non-empty vector representation

name: single_entry_vec_length_exceeds_one
code:
proof fn phi_2_single_entry_vec_length_exceeds_one(m: CKeyHashMap, k: AbstractKey, v: Seq<u8>)
    requires
        m@ === Map::<AbstractKey, Seq<u8>>::empty().insert(k, v),
    ensures
        m.spec_to_vec()@.len() > 1,
{
}
reason: A map with exactly one entry should not produce a vector with more than one element

name: equal_view_different_vec_len
code:
proof fn phi_3_equal_view_different_vec_len(m1: CKeyHashMap, m2: CKeyHashMap)
    requires
        m1@ === m2@,
    ensures
        m1.spec_to_vec()@.len() != m2.spec_to_vec()@.len(),
{
}
reason: Maps with identical abstract views must produce vectors of the same length; differing lengths would break the bijection

name: vec_key_not_in_domain
code:
proof fn phi_4_vec_key_not_in_domain(m: CKeyHashMap, i: int)
    requires
        0 <= i < m.spec_to_vec()@.len(),
    ensures
        !m@.dom().contains(m.spec_to_vec()@[i].k),
{
}
reason: Every key appearing in the vector representation should belong to the map's domain

name: duplicate_keys_in_vec
code:
proof fn phi_5_duplicate_keys_in_vec(m: CKeyHashMap, i: int, j: int)
    requires
        0 <= i < m.spec_to_vec()@.len(),
        0 <= j < m.spec_to_vec()@.len(),
        i != j,
    ensures
        m.spec_to_vec()@[i].k === m.spec_to_vec()@[j].k,
{
}
reason: A faithful map-to-vector conversion must not contain duplicate keys since maps have unique keys
